title: EPF Withdrawal — Complete Guide
meta_description: EPF withdrawal 2026 — how to withdraw PF money online, advance withdrawal rules for home/medical/marriage, Form 19/31/10C, and UAN activation guide.

hero.one_liner: How to withdraw your Employees' Provident Fund money — online process, advance withdrawal for specific needs, and rules for full settlement after leaving a job

sections.heading: Overview
content: Employees' Provident Fund (EPF) is the retirement savings that both you and your employer contribute to during your employment — 12% of your basic salary from you + 12% from your employer. The money accumulates in your EPF account earning 8.25% interest (2025-26 rate) and is meant for retirement. However, you can withdraw it partially or fully under specific conditions.

Full withdrawal (Form 19 + Form 10C): You can withdraw the entire EPF balance after leaving your job and remaining unemployed for 2 consecutive months. If you've completed 5 or more years of continuous service (across employers if EPF was transferred), the withdrawal is completely tax-free. If less than 5 years, TDS of 10% is deducted (no TDS if amount is below ₹50,000).

Advance/Partial withdrawal (Form 31): While still employed, you can withdraw a portion of your EPF for specific purposes: purchase/construction of house, home loan repayment, medical treatment, marriage (self/children/siblings), education of children, or within 1 year before retirement. Each purpose has specific conditions and limits on how much you can withdraw.

The entire process is now online through the EPFO Member Portal (unifiedportal-mem.epfindia.gov.in). No need to visit the PF office. Your UAN (Universal Account Number) must be activated and linked to your Aadhaar, PAN, and bank account for online withdrawal. Processing typically takes 10-20 days for the amount to reach your bank account.

sections.heading: How to Withdraw EPF Online — Step by Step

steps.title: Activate your UAN (if not already done)
steps.description: Visit unifiedportal-mem.epfindia.gov.in → 'Activate UAN' → Enter UAN, Aadhaar/PAN, name, DOB, mobile → Verify with OTP → Set password. Your UAN is on your payslip or you can ask HR.

steps.title: Ensure KYC is verified
steps.description: Login to EPFO portal → 'Manage' → 'KYC' → Verify that Aadhaar, PAN, and Bank Account are linked and status shows 'Verified'. If not verified, submit them and wait for employer approval (2-5 days). Aadhaar must be linked to receive online withdrawal.

steps.title: Submit online claim
steps.description: Login → 'Online Services' → 'Claim (Form 31, 19, 10C)' → Select claim type: Form 31 for advance withdrawal (while employed), Form 19 for full withdrawal (after leaving job). → Enter amount and purpose → Verify with Aadhaar OTP → Submit.

steps.title: Track claim and receive money
steps.description: Check status at: Login → 'Track Claim Status'. Normal processing: 10-20 days. Money is transferred directly to your Aadhaar-linked bank account via NEFT/RTGS. If rejected, the portal shows the reason — fix and resubmit.

sections.heading: Tax Rules on EPF Withdrawal
content: 5+ years of continuous service: Withdrawal is completely tax-free. This is the ideal scenario. Service years across multiple employers count if you transferred your EPF (not opened a new account) when changing jobs.

Less than 5 years: TDS at 10% is deducted if withdrawal exceeds ₹50,000 and PAN is linked. If PAN is not linked, TDS is 20%. You can claim refund when filing ITR if your total income is below taxable limit.

Transfer instead of withdrawal: When changing jobs, ALWAYS transfer EPF instead of withdrawing. Transfer is: (a) tax-free regardless of years, (b) maintains continuity of service for the 5-year rule, (c) your money keeps earning 8.25% interest. Transfer is done online through the same EPFO portal → 'One Member One EPF Account' transfer request.

Interest on inactive EPF: If you leave a job and don't withdraw or transfer your EPF for 3+ years, the account becomes 'inoperative'. An inoperative EPF account stops earning interest. Always transfer to new employer's EPF or withdraw within 3 years of leaving.

sections.heading: Common EPF Withdrawal Problems and Solutions
content: Problem: 'UAN not activated' — Contact your current or last employer's HR to get your UAN. If employer is unresponsive, visit the regional EPFO office with your payslips and Aadhaar for manual activation.

Problem: 'KYC not verified by employer' — Your employer must digitally approve your KYC (Aadhaar/PAN/Bank). If they're slow, follow up with HR. If employer has shut down, you can get KYC verified at the EPFO office with original documents.

Problem: 'Claim rejected — name mismatch' — Your name must match exactly across Aadhaar, PAN, bank account, and EPF records. Even minor differences (middle name, spelling) cause rejection. Get corrections done at the source (Aadhaar/bank) and resubmit.

Problem: 'Previous employer PF not showing in UAN' — If you have multiple Member IDs from different employers, they need to be linked to one UAN. Login → 'One Member One EPF Account' → submit linking request. Or visit EPFO office.
Problem: 'Claim stuck for more than 30 days' — File a grievance at epfigms.gov.in. EPFO is mandated to respond within 30 days. If still unresolved, escalate to the PF Regional Commissioner's office.

steps.title: Login to EPFO Member Portal
steps.description: unifiedportal-mem.epfindia.gov.in → Login with UAN + password. Ensure Aadhaar, PAN, and Bank KYC is verified.

steps.title: Go to Online Services → Claim
steps.description: Select Form 19 (full settlement after leaving job) or Form 31 (advance while employed). Choose purpose for Form 31.

steps.title: Enter details and submit with Aadhaar OTP
steps.description: Fill claim amount, verify bank details, and authenticate with Aadhaar-linked OTP.

steps.title: Track status and receive in 10-20 days
steps.description: Money credited to bank via NEFT/RTGS. Track at portal → 'Track Claim Status'.

faqs.question: Can I withdraw EPF while still employed?
faqs.answer: Yes — advance withdrawal (Form 31) is allowed while employed, but only for specific purposes: house purchase/construction, home loan repayment, medical treatment (hospitalization 1+ month), marriage, education, or if you're 54+ (within 1 year of retirement). Each purpose has minimum service requirements and withdrawal limits. You cannot withdraw the full amount while employed.

faqs.question: How long does EPF withdrawal take?
faqs.answer: Online claims with verified KYC: 10-20 working days. If submitted offline through employer: 20-40 days. Delays usually happen due to: unverified KYC, name mismatches, employer not attesting the claim, or high volume at the EPFO office. File grievance at epfigms.gov.in if it exceeds 30 days.

faqs.question: Can I withdraw my employer's contribution?
faqs.answer: Yes. When you submit Form 19 for full withdrawal after leaving job, you get both your contribution AND employer's contribution (minus the amount diverted to EPS pension scheme). For the EPS part, submit Form 10C for withdrawal benefit (if less than 10 years of service) or opt for pension (if 10+ years of service).

faqs.question: I left my job 5 years ago and never withdrew PF. Is it still there?
faqs.answer: Yes, the money is still in your EPF account. However, after 3 years of inactivity (no contributions), the account becomes 'inoperative' and stops earning interest. You can still withdraw it — login to EPFO portal or visit the regional office. For very old accounts (pre-UAN era), you may need to visit EPFO office with original payslips and employer details.

faqs.question: EPF vs VPF — what's the difference?
faqs.answer: EPF: Mandatory 12% of basic salary contribution (you + employer). VPF (Voluntary Provident Fund): You can voluntarily contribute MORE than 12% (up to 100% of basic). VPF earns the same 8.25% interest and has the same tax benefits. It's one of the best debt investments available — higher return than FD/PPF, with tax-free withdrawal after 5 years. If you have surplus salary, increase VPF instead of opening new FDs.

disclaimer: This content is for educational purposes only. Consult a qualified financial advisor before making investment decisions.
